entry.title: Import external service odata v4 , Error : No metadata factory provided
entry.content:
After generating my CAP project, I want to import and use external service odata (OP or Private Cloud). If I download an edmx odata v2 file from sap business hub api or from the AS, the command “cds import myedmx.edmx” works correctly.

However, if I take an edmx file from an odata v4 service from https://api.sap.com/api/OP_PRODUCT_0002/overview or https://api.sap.com/api/OP_PURCHASEORDER_0001/overview

or directly from AS

I got this error:

cds import ..\OP_PRODUCT_0002.edmx                 
Error: No metadata factory provided. Can not resolve 'com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1'. Please add one via Strategy.setMetadataFactory(function(type, callback){})
    at C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:1095:22
    at Context._getEdm (C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:936:51)
    at C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:986:25
    at new Promise (<anonymous>)
    at Context.resolveType (C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:983:16)     
    at C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:1787:22
    at Context._emit (C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:185:21)
    at C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:186:30
    at C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\lib\import\odata\metadataconverter\MetadataConverter.js:1793:32

If i try to delete tag "'com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1'" in metadata, i have same error but in another tag.

Specs :

VScode, Node 20.18.1(same behavior with Node 22)

  "dependencies": {
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "^3.25.0",
    "@sap/cds": "^8.6.0",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^8.6.1",
    "express": "^4"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-types": "^0.8.0",
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^1"
  },

entry.title: Step By Step Guide to Transport Table Contents from One Environment to Other in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Transporting table contents in SAP S/4HANA should be approached with caution, especially for large tables or tables containing transactional data. Direct table content transports are generally discouraged for production systems due to potential data inconsistencies. However, for smaller tables, configuration tables, or development/testing purposes, there are methods. Here's a step-by-step guide focusing on the recommended approaches and highlighting the risks:

Method 1: Using Transport of Copies (Recommended for Configuration and Small Tables):

This is the most common and generally recommended approach for transporting table contents. It's suitable for configuration tables or small amounts of master data.

  1. Create a Transport Request: In the source system (development or quality), use transaction SE01 or SE10 to create a new transport request.

  2. Add Table Contents to the Transport Request:

    • Use transaction SM30 (Maintain Table) or SE16N (Data Browser).
    • Enter the table name.
    • Select the entries you want to transport (or all entries if needed, but be cautious with large tables).
    • In SM30, you can use the "Transport" function (usually under "Table" -> "Transport"). In SE16N, use the "Table Content Transport" function (usually under "Utilities" -> "Table Content Transport").
    • Assign the transport request you created in step 1.

  3. Release the Transport Request: In the source system, use transaction SE01 or SE10 to release the transport request. This exports the table contents to transport files.

  4. Import the Transport Request: In the target system (quality or production, with extreme caution for production), use transaction STMS (Transport Management System) to import the transport request.

Method 2: Using SAP Data Services (For Large Data Volumes and Complex Scenarios):

For large tables or complex data transformations, SAP Data Services is the recommended solution. It offers robust data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) capabilities.

  1. Design a Data Services Job: Create a Data Services job that extracts data from the source table.
  2. Transform the Data (If Necessary): Apply any required data transformations or cleansing rules.
  3. Load the Data into the Target Table: Configure the job to load the data into the corresponding table in the target system.
  4. Execute the Data Services Job: Run the job to transfer the data.

Method 3: Using ABAP Programs (For Specific Requirements and Complex Logic):

For highly specific requirements or complex logic, you can develop an ABAP program to extract and insert data.

  1. Develop an ABAP Program: Create an ABAP program that reads data from the source table using SELECT statements and inserts it into the target table using INSERT statements.
  2. Create a Transport Request: Add the ABAP program to a transport request.
  3. Transport and Execute the Program: Transport the request to the target system and execute the program.

Important Considerations and Cautions:

  • Consistency: Transporting table contents directly can lead to data inconsistencies if related data in other tables is not transported. Ensure data consistency before and after the transport.
  • Dependencies: Be aware of dependencies between tables. If you transport data from one table, you might need to transport data from related tables as well.
  • Large Tables: Avoid transporting large tables directly, especially in production systems. This can cause performance issues and downtime. Use SAP Data Services or other ETL tools for large data volumes.
  • Transactional Data: Do not transport transactional data (e.g., sales orders, purchase orders) using these methods. Use standard SAP processes like ALE/IDoc or other integration technologies.
  • Production Systems: Exercise extreme caution when transporting table contents to production systems. Thoroughly test the transport in a non-production environment first. Always have a backup of the target system before importing any table content transports.
  • Use Standard SAP Functionality Whenever Possible: Before resorting to direct table transports, explore standard SAP functionalities like customizing transports, master data transports, or application-specific data transfer tools.

Example (Method 1 - SM30):

Let's say you want to transport entries from table T001W (Plants):

  1. SE01 - Create a transport request.
  2. SM30 - Enter T001W.
  3. Click "Maintain."
  4. Select the plant entries you want to transport.
  5. Go to "Table" -> "Transport."
  6. Assign your transport request.
  7. SE01 - Release the transport request.
  8. STMS - Import the transport request into the target system.

By following these steps and considering the important cautions, you can transport table contents safely and effectively in SAP S/4HANA for appropriate use cases. Remember to prioritize data consistency and avoid direct table transports in production for transactional data or large volumes.

entry.title: General tables useful in the SAP S4HANA module MM
entry.content:
The Material Management (MM) module in SAP S/4HANA uses a simplified data model compared to older versions. While some classic tables remain for compatibility, new tables and structures are central to S/4HANA's architecture. Here's a breakdown of commonly used tables, keeping in mind the S/4HANA context:

1. Material Master Data:

  • MARA: General Material Data (basic information like material type, material group, etc.)
  • MAKT: Material Descriptions (descriptions in different languages)
  • MARM: Units of Measure for Material (quantity units, dimensions, etc.)
  • MBEW: Material Valuation (accounting and valuation data)
  • MARD: Storage Location Data for Material (stock quantities per storage location)

2. Purchasing:

  • EKKO: Purchasing Document Header (general information about the purchase order)
  • EKPO: Purchasing Document Item (details of each item in the order)
  • EKET: Scheduling Agreements (delivery schedule dates)
  • EINA: Purchasing Info Record - General Data (general information about the vendor-material relationship)
  • EINE: Purchasing Info Record - Purchasing Organization Data (data specific to the purchasing organization)
  • EBAN: Purchase Requisition (internal request for procurement)

3. Inventory Management (Key Change: MATDOC):

  • MATDOC: This is the most important table for inventory management in S/4HANA. It replaces the classic combination of MKPF (header) and MSEG (item) tables. MATDOC stores all material document information in a single table, significantly improving performance. This is a crucial change in S/4HANA.
    • MKPF and MSEG still exist, primarily for compatibility with older reports and programs. However, for new development and reporting, MATDOC should be used.
  • MBLNR: Material Document Number (still relevant for referencing documents).
  • BWART: Movement Type (identifies the type of stock movement).
  • LGORT: Storage Location.
  • MATNR: Material Number.

4. Valuation:

  • MBEW: Material Valuation (accounting and valuation data, standard price, moving average price, etc.)
  • CKMI: Price History (history of price changes)

5. Material Requirements Planning (MRP):

  • MDKP: MRP Header Data (general information about the MRP run)
  • MDTB: Planning Table (results of the requirements calculation)

6. General and Organizational Data:

  • T001W: Plants
  • T024E: Purchasing Organizations
  • T001L: Storage Locations

7. Pricing Conditions (Important Change: PRCD_ELEMENTS):

  • PRCD_ELEMENTS: This is the central pricing table in S/4HANA. It stores pricing element details (discounts, surcharges, taxes) for various business processes (sales, purchasing, billing). This table improves performance and flexibility compared to older condition tables.
    • KONV, KONP, and condition tables (A001-AXXX) still exist for compatibility but are less central in S/4HANA.

Key S/4HANA Considerations:

  • Simplified Data Model: S/4HANA aims to reduce data redundancy. MATDOC and PRCD_ELEMENTS are prime examples of this simplification.
  • Compatibility Views: SAP provides compatibility views to map old table structures to new ones. This allows older programs to continue working.
  • CDS Views: Core Data Services (CDS) views are heavily used for data access and reporting. They provide a virtual data model on top of the physical tables, simplifying data consumption. This is the preferred way to access data in S/4HANA.
  • Business Partner (BP): While not a table itself, the Business Partner is a key concept in S/4HANA. It replaces vendor master (LFA1, LFB1, etc.) and customer master data, using tables like BUT000.

In summary: While some classic MM tables remain, S/4HANA introduces key changes with tables like MATDOC and PRCD_ELEMENTS, along with the Business Partner concept and CDS views. When working in an S/4HANA system, focus on understanding these new structures and using CDS views for data access whenever possible. This will ensure better performance and alignment with S/4HANA's architecture.

entry.title: Tables générales utilisées par le module MM de SAP
entry.content:
Le module MM (Material Management) de SAP est vaste et utilise de nombreuses tables pour stocker les données relatives à la gestion des articles, des achats, des stocks, etc. Voici une liste des tables générales les plus utilisées dans le module MM, classées par domaine pour plus de clarté :

1. Données de base articles (Material Master):

  • MARA : Données générales articles (informations de base comme le type d'article, le groupe de marchandises, etc.)
  • MAKT : Désignations des articles (descriptions en différentes langues)
  • MARM : Unités de mesure pour l'article (unités de quantité, dimensions, etc.)
  • MBEW : Valorisation article (données comptables et de valorisation)
  • MARD : Données de stockage article par magasin (quantités en stock par emplacement)

2. Achats (Purchasing):

  • EKKO : En-tête de document d'achat (informations générales sur la commande d'achat)
  • EKPO : Poste de document d'achat (détails de chaque position de la commande)
  • EKET : Échéances du programme de livraison (dates de livraison prévues)
  • EINA : Fiche infos-achats - données générales (informations générales sur la relation fournisseur-article)
  • EINE : Fiche infos-ach. - données de l'organisation d'achat (données spécifiques à l'organisation d'achat)
  • EBAN : Demande d'achat (requête interne pour l'approvisionnement)

3. Mouvements de stock (Inventory Management):

  • MKPF : En-tête du document article (informations générales sur le mouvement de stock)
  • MSEG : Segment de document pour article (détails de chaque mouvement de stock)
  • MBLNR : Numéro de document article.
  • BWART : Type de mouvement.
  • LGORT : Emplacement de stockage.
  • MATNR : Numéro d'article.

4. Valorisation (Valuation):

  • MBEW : Valorisation article (données comptables et de valorisation, prix standard, prix moyen mobile, etc.)
  • CKMI : Historique des prix (historique des modifications de prix)

5. Planification des besoins (Material Requirements Planning - MRP):

  • MDKP : Données en-tête document de planification (informations générales sur l'exécution MRP)
  • MDTB : Table de planification (résultats du calcul des besoins)

6. Données générales et organisations:

  • T001W : Tables des usines
  • T024E : Organisations d'achat
  • T001L : Emplacements de stockage

Tables importantes pour les conditions de prix:

  • KONV : Conditions (données des conditions de prix)
  • KONP : Poste de condition (détails de chaque condition)
  • A001 à AXXX : Tables de conditions (dépendent du type de condition)
  • PRCD_ELEMENTS : Table de prix dans S/4HANA (simplifie la structure des données de prix)

Remarques importantes :

  • Cette liste n'est pas exhaustive, mais elle couvre les tables les plus couramment utilisées.
  • Dans S/4HANA, certaines tables ont été remplacées ou fusionnées pour simplifier le modèle de données. Par exemple, la table MATDOC remplace la combinaison de MKPF et MSEG pour les documents articles.
  • L'accès direct aux tables en production est généralement déconseillé. Il est préférable d'utiliser les transactions et les rapports standard de SAP ou de créer des requêtes personnalisées via des vues CDS (Core Data Services) dans S/4HANA.
  • La structure des tables et les noms des champs peuvent varier légèrement en fonction de la version de SAP utilisée.

Pour une recherche plus spécifique, il est utile de connaître la transaction ou le processus métier concerné. Par exemple, si vous recherchez les tables utilisées pour la réception de marchandises, vous pouvez vous concentrer sur les tables MKPF et MSEG.

En utilisant ces informations, vous devriez être en mesure de mieux comprendre la structure des données dans le module MM de SAP. N'hésitez pas à poser d'autres questions si vous avez besoin de précisions.

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Packs for Simple Finance
entry.content:
Hi, what exacly you mean with "Compatibility Packs" ? This is only relevant for functionality which still can be used at SAP S/4HANA till end of 2025.

I guess you are looking for SAP Document Reporting Compliance.

There are many notes and help pages, not just one. It depends what exactly, you are looking for.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3cc1da1e1e364fadb9b1af653a9597d2/8f7ba057f06c3925e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

you can check the available reports here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3b2c091e13434f9ab2fb53d147ba5e9f/097165e9c1074038847625e5c53e07d2.html?locale=en-US&version=2023.001

also 2480067 - Replacement of Existing Legal Reports with 'SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - Statutory Reports' 

SAP S/4HANA 2021 is quite old. You should definitely plan an upgrade to SAP S/4HANA 2023 next year!

Regards

Thomas

entry.title: New MM tables in SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, there's a significant shift towards simplifying the data model, particularly in Materials Management (MM). This involves consolidating data and introducing new tables to improve performance and reduce redundancy. While some traditional tables are still present for compatibility, new tables and data structures are central to S/4HANA's architecture.

Here are some key changes and new MM tables in SAP S/4HANA, with a focus on how they relate to the older structure and the example of PRCD_ELEMENTS:

1. Material Document (Inventory Management):

  • MATDOC: This is a crucial new table. It replaces the traditional combination of MKPF (header) and MSEG (item) tables for material documents. MATDOC stores all the information in a single table with a simplified structure, significantly improving performance for inventory reporting and analysis.
    • It uses a "universal journal" approach, similar to ACDOCA in Finance, storing both header and item data in one place.
    • MATDOC includes fields for various applications, reducing the need to join multiple tables.

2. Pricing Conditions:

  • PRCD_ELEMENTS: You specifically mentioned this table. It's a key table for pricing conditions in S/4HANA. It stores the details of pricing elements, such as discounts, surcharges, and taxes, for various business processes, including sales, purchasing, and billing.
    • This table is designed for better performance and flexibility in pricing compared to older condition tables like KONV.
    • It's used extensively in pricing procedures and condition techniques.

3. Business Partner:

  • BUT000: While not strictly an MM table, the Business Partner concept is fundamental in S/4HANA. It replaces vendor master (LFA1, LFB1, etc.) and customer master data. This single point of entry for master data simplifies data maintenance and reduces redundancy.

4. Other Important Changes and Considerations:

  • Simplified Data Model: S/4HANA aims to reduce data redundancy and improve performance by consolidating tables and using more efficient data structures.
  • Compatibility Views: To maintain compatibility with existing programs and reports, SAP provides compatibility views that map the old table structures to the new ones. This allows older programs to continue functioning without requiring immediate changes.
  • CDS Views: Core Data Services (CDS) views are heavily used in S/4HANA for data access and reporting. These views provide a virtual data model on top of the physical tables, simplifying data consumption and enabling more flexible reporting options.

Example: How PRCD_ELEMENTS Improves Pricing:

In older SAP systems, pricing information was spread across multiple tables like KONV, KONP, KONH, etc. This made it complex to retrieve and analyze pricing data. PRCD_ELEMENTS centralizes this information, making it easier to:

  • Access pricing details for various business processes.
  • Analyze pricing conditions and their impact on profitability.
  • Develop custom pricing solutions and reports.

In summary: While some traditional MM tables still exist for compatibility, S/4HANA introduces new tables like MATDOC and PRCD_ELEMENTS to simplify the data model, improve performance, and provide more flexibility. The Business Partner concept and the use of CDS views further enhance data management and reporting capabilities in S/4HANA.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA MM supplier invoice flexible workflow
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA MM supplier invoice flexible workflow allows you to automate and streamline the invoice approval process based on predefined rules and conditions. While the term "stochastic block" isn't directly used within the standard SAP documentation for this workflow, it's possible to relate the concept to how the workflow handles certain situations.

Here's how the flexible workflow works and where the idea of "stochastic" or probabilistic behavior might come into play:

How the Flexible Workflow Works:

  1. Configuration: You define workflows with specific steps and conditions for approval. These conditions can be based on factors like:

    • Invoice amount
    • Vendor
    • Company code
    • Material group
    • Cost center

  2. Workflow Initiation: When a supplier invoice is entered into the system, the workflow is triggered.

  3. Step Determination: The system evaluates the conditions defined in the workflow to determine the appropriate approval steps and approvers.

  4. Approval Process: The invoice is routed to the designated approvers, who can approve or reject it.

  5. Automated Actions: Based on the approval or rejection, the system can automatically perform actions like posting the invoice or sending notifications.

Relating to "Stochastic Block":

The term "stochastic" implies randomness or probability. In the context of the flexible workflow, this could relate to situations where:

  • Multiple Approvers: If multiple approvers are assigned to a step, the workflow might be configured to require approval from a certain percentage or a random selection of those approvers. This introduces a probabilistic element to the approval process.

  • Dynamic Approver Determination: The workflow could use rules that dynamically determine approvers based on factors that might have some variability, such as current organizational assignments or employee availability.

  • Escalation Rules: If an approver doesn't take action within a certain time, the workflow might escalate the invoice to a higher-level approver. The timing of this escalation could have some flexibility or be based on predefined intervals, introducing a time-based probabilistic element.

Example:

Let's say you have a workflow where invoices over $10,000 require approval from two out of three assigned cost center managers. This introduces a "stochastic" element because the system might randomly select which two managers need to approve the invoice.

Key Takeaways:

  • The SAP S/4HANA MM supplier invoice flexible workflow is primarily deterministic, meaning it follows predefined rules and conditions.
  • However, certain configurations, such as multiple approvers or dynamic approver determination, can introduce elements of randomness or probability, which could be loosely related to the concept of a "stochastic block."
  • The focus of the workflow is on automation and efficiency, ensuring that invoices are approved by the right people based on defined criteria.

If you have specific scenarios or configurations in mind, providing more details would help in giving a more precise explanation of how the flexible workflow behaves in those situations.

entry.title: Re: Invalid ## characters showing in user menu in SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
SAP notes with titles and excerpts, to apply only the note 2068736 if below Basis 755, or only the note 3413131 if Basis 755 or above:

  • 2068736 - Invalid characters (e.g. #) shown in the user menu - SAP for Me (solution excerpt: "As the solution of note 3413131 is available as of Basis 755 (S/4 2020) only, please follow [...] below procedure, if the system is below that release", which means that, as of Basis 755, the note 2068736 is to be ignored and replaced by the note 3413131; if below Basis 755, in the attachments, there are two Z programs to install and run).
  • 3413131 - SEC_SUPPORT: Revision of texts in the role menu - SAP for Me (symptom excerpt: "In customer systems, you sometimes notice missing menu texts and menu texts with # characters"; solution excerpt: "After you implement this solution, the new report RPRGN_UPD_ROLE_MENU_TXT [...]")

entry.title: Item Category Groups
entry.content:
What is an Item Category Group?

The Item Category Group is a key control element in SAP SD that classifies materials based on how they should be handled in sales documents (like sales orders, deliveries, and billing documents). It's a crucial link between the material master and the sales document processing.

Key Functions of the Item Category Group:

Item Category Determination: This is the primary function. The system uses the Item Category Group in conjunction with the Sales Document Type and Item Category Usage to determine the appropriate Item Category. The Item Category then controls various aspects of the item within the sales document.

Control of Business Processes: The Item Category Group influences several processes, including:

Pricing: As you mentioned, it can determine whether pricing is relevant for an item (e.g., free goods are usually not priced).

Inventory Management: It controls whether goods movements are posted upon delivery (relevant for stock items but not for services).

Billing Relevance: It determines whether an item is relevant for billing.

Delivery Relevance: It determines whether an item requires a delivery.

Schedule Line Category Determination: It influences the schedule line category, which controls delivery scheduling and availability checks.

Account Assignment: It can influence how costs and revenues are assigned to general ledger accounts.

How Item Category Determination Works:

The system uses a combination of three factors to determine the Item Category:

Sales Document Type: (e.g., OR - Standard Order, TA - Third-Party Order)

Item Category Group: (e.g., NORM - Standard Item, DIEN - Service, LEIS - Free of Charge)

Item Category Usage: (This is less commonly used but provides further differentiation)

This combination is defined in a table (usually TVAP) which the system consults during sales document processing.

Examples of Item Category Groups and Their Usage:

NORM (Standard Item): Used for most standard sales items that require pricing, inventory management, and delivery.

DIEN (Service): Used for services that do not require inventory management or physical delivery.

LEIS (Free of Charge Item): Used for free goods or samples that are not priced and may or may not require delivery.

NLAG (Non-Stock Item): Used for items that are procured directly from a vendor for a specific sales order and are not held in stock.

VERP (Packaging): Used for packaging materials that are often delivered with the main product.

Example Scenario:

Let's say you create a sales order (Sales Document Type OR) for a standard product (Item Category Group NORM). The system, based on the configuration, might determine the Item Category TAN (Standard Item). This Item Category TAN then controls various aspects of the item, such as pricing, delivery, and billing.

If you create a sales order for a service (Item Category Group DIEN), the system might determine the Item Category DIEN, which would prevent inventory updates and control the billing process differently.

In Summary:

The Item Category Group is a powerful tool for controlling how materials are processed in sales documents. By correctly assigning Item Category Groups to materials, you can ensure that sales processes are executed efficiently and accurately. It is a fundamental concept in SAP SD and is crucial for proper sales order processing.

entry.title: Customizing Your PDF with the Transparency Information Required by the Argentinean Tax Authority
entry.content:
Hello! 
 
If you carry out sales, work placements or provision of services to final consumers in Argentina using print character B, according to resolution 5614, as of January 1st, 2025, you must indicate the final amount that the final consumer must pay in the publication of the prices of the respective goods or services.

According to the tax authority, this information is displayed in a new box containing the following information:  

Title 

Description 

Régimen de Transparencia Fiscal al Consumidor (Ley 27.743) 

Fixed title 

IVA Contenido $/USD 

Total VAT of the document in document currency (use the symbol $ for local currency or ISO currency code for foreign currencies like USD).  

In case you have more than one VAT rate in the document, this line should show the summarization of all IVA amounts. 

Otros Impuestos Nacionales Indirectos $/USD 

Total of other national taxes (not VAT), for example Internal tax (use the symbol $ for local currency or ISO currency code for foreign currencies like USD). In SAP, we have identified this kind of tax as “internal tax” 

Note that Otros Impuestos Nacionales Indirectos are not applied to all companies. For more information, see Transparencia fiscal: ARCA reglamenta el régimen que obliga a discriminar el IVA en las facturas and Resolución General 5614/2024. 

With this blog post, we will guide you with examples on how to comply with this legal change if you use PDF forms in the eDocument Cockpit for Argentina with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.  

Please note that the steps mentioned are suggestions, indications, and examples. You may use your own logic if what is provided does not apply to your needs. 

Prerequisites:

You have Adobe LiveCycle Designer on your computer and know how to design forms with this tool. 

You have assigned the Extensibility (SAP_CORE_BC_EXT) authorization role for your user. 

Procedure:

  1. Access the Maintain Form Templates app. 
  2. Go to the Predelivered Templates tab and search for the following PDF form: 
    Document Type:Print Char A, B, E
    Form Template Name:EDO_AR_PRINT
    Data Source:EDOC_AR_PRINT_SRV_03
  3. Open the form template and select Download. 
  4. Extract the downloaded ZIP file and open the .xdp file in the Adobe Livecycle Desginer. 
  5. In the Adobe LifeCycle Designer, choose the Master Pages tab.  
  6. From the Object Library, insert a Text Field to give the table its title, Régimen de Transparencia Fiscal al Consumidor. 
  7. From the Object Library, insert a table to your form by selecting Table. 
  8. Create a simple table with 2 columns and 2 rows. 
  9. Name the rows by double-clicking a cell. Consider the required texts, as follows:
    • First row: IVA Contenido 
    • Second row: Otros Impuestos Nacionales Indirectos

  10. To fill in the information for IVA Contenido, perform as follows:  
  1. Double-click the respective cell and navigate from Object > Cell and set its type as Text Field.  
  2. To bind the data, you have different options, choose the one that best suits your scenario.  
  • Use Javascript logic to get your specific values.
  • Choose the Binding tab and, in the Data Binding field, paste the following:  $.HEADER.VATSet.VAT[0].Amount  
    The [0] index means you are getting the first row of the referenced table. 

11. The value for Otros Impuestos Nacionales is already available in the Otros Tributos table. To retrieve the value, perform as follows: 

  1. Double-click the respective cell and navigate from Object > Cell and set its type as Text Field. 
  2. To bind the data, you have different options, choose the one best suits your scenario: 
  • Choose the Binding tab and, in the Data Binding field, paste the following: $.HEADER.OTHER_TAXSet.OTHER_TAX[0].Amount  

  • Use Javascript logic to get your specific values, for example:  

var description = this.resolveNode("Page1.other_taxset.item_other_tax[0].description").rawValue; 

  

if(description == 'Impto.interno ventas'){ 
  this.rawValue = this.resolveNode("Page1.other_taxset.item_other_tax[0].amount").rawValue; 
} 

12. Save your changes. 
13. Access the Maintain Form Templates app. 
14. On the Custom Templates tab, choose New. Create a custom template for your form by making the following settings: 

  • Form Template Name: Enter a name for your form template. 
  • Language: Select the language of your form template. 
  • Select Form Template File: Upload the file that you've created in Adobe LiveCycle Designer. 
  • Data Source: EDOC_AR_PRINT_SRV_03 
  • Form Type: Choose Content. 

15. Save your form template. 
16. In your configuration environment, use the search function to open the Configure Printing Form for eInvoice (103277) configuration activity and assign your custom form template to the relevant eDocument type and branch.  

You can now open the eDocument Cockpit and submit your document. The new PDF form template now contains the Régimen de Transparencia Fiscal al Consumidor (Ley 27.743) box and its information.

We hope that you find this information useful. Feel free to leave a comment on this blog or follow SAP Document and Reporting Compliance | SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Lessons Learned: Deploy app on HTML5 Repository
entry.content:
@Cocquerel , the benefits of using HTML5 Repository are that you take less resources of your Cloud Foundry because the HTML5 Repo don't use Memory, Routes, etc. from your subaccount. It's also easier to add them to a Launchpad, a Work Zone, where you can use the Personalization features, restrict access by Role, and so on.

On the other hand, it's indeed less flexible than creating your own App Router, where you have access to the approuter code.

I'd say it's a tradeoff between ease of deploying and flexibility of configuration.

entry.title: 🇳🇱 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday February 14th, 2025 at the INNOV8iON offices in Utrecht, Netherlands 🇳🇱 from 13:00 - 19:00 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

The instructor will be Developer Advocate @noravonthenen.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Conquering the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification: A Preparation Guide
entry.content:
This blog post provides a comprehensive guide to help you effectively prepare for the P_BTPA_2408 certification:

1.Understand the Exam Blueprint:

  • Thoroughly review the official exam blueprint. This document outlines the exam objectives, topics, and weighting.

    Exam code : P_BTPA_2408

    Questions : 40 (single and multiple choice )

    Time: 3 hours

    Cut-off : 68%

    visit the link for more information 

    https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-professional-solution-architect-sap-btp

    • Focus on key areas: Pay close attention to areas like:
      • Core BTP Services: Deep dive into services like SAP Integration Suite, SAP Data Intelligence , SAP BTP Available environment, SAP AI Core,destination/Connectivity,API management ,Cloud Identity service 
      • Architecture and Design Principles: Understand BTP's architecture, design patterns, and best practices for building robust and scalable solutions.All Architecture(Data/Integration/Extension ) methodology explained in the course 
      • Account model : Learn about available account model in BTP
      • Integration Scenarios: Explore various integration scenarios, including on-premise to cloud, cloud-to-cloud, and B2B integrations.
      • Development options available: Understanding of all available option for LCNC ,Build family comparison ,AI Core service

2.Leverage Official SAP Resources:

  • SAP Learning Hub: Utilize the SAP Learning Hub for access courses, tutorials, and practice exams.

    https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-an-sap-btp-solution-architect

    If you go through available course content at learning hub,it will provide you a solid root for exam preparation

  • SAP Help Portal: Explore the extensive documentation and help resources available on the SAP Help Portal for go deeper on BTP available service
  • SAP Discovery center https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/index.html 
  •  SAP Business Accelerator Hub : https://api.sap.com/
  • SAP Community: Engage with the SAP Community forums to connect with other professionals, ask questions, and share knowledge.

3.Hands-on Experience:

  • Gain practical experience: Work on hands-on exercises, labs, and projects to solidify your understanding of BTP concepts.

    You can explore available BTP trail account to explore various service and other development/security related options

    https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-create-trial-account..html

    • Build sample applications: Develop simple applications using BTP services to gain practical experience and reinforce your learning.
    • Explore real-world scenarios: Analyze real-world business challenges and design BTP-based solutions to address them,As in the exam there are many questions on real world scenarios and you have to select the best possible answer for that.

4. Study and Practice Consistently:

Create a study plan: Allocate dedicated time for consistent study and review.

Use flashcards and mind maps: To summarize key concepts and aid in memorization.Specially to understand different methodology and available Build options

Review and revise: Regularly review previously studied material to reinforce your understanding.

5.Seek Guidance and Support:

  • Connect with mentors or experienced SAP professionals: Seek guidance from experienced colleagues or mentors who have successfully passed the certification.
  • Join study groups: Collaborate with other candidates to share knowledge, discuss challenging topics, and stay motivated.

6.Exam Day Tips:

  • Read each question carefully: Ensure you understand the question and all its parts before answering.
  • Time: Available time is more than sufficient for the exam
  • Test Readiness : After booking the exam please check the exam guideline and preparation instructions carefully.
  • Review your answers: You can mark questions for review during the exam and review your answers before submitting the exam.

    Conclusion:

    The P_BTPA_2408 certification requires dedication and effort. By following these guidelines and consistently working towards your goals, you can increase your chances of success and gain valuable expertise in the SAP BTP ecosystem.

    Disclaimer: This blog post provides general guidance and should not be considered an official or guaranteed method for exam success. Always refer to the official SAP documentation and resources for the most up-to-date information.

    I hope this blog post helps you in your preparation for the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification!

    HappY Learning 

    Thanks & Best Regards,

    Atul Kumar Jain

entry.title: "No root key matching hash" when recovering HANA Database with encrypted backup
entry.content:
Symptom :

We were performing the system refresh activity and production HANA Database version is HANA 2.0 SPS07 with this version encryption is enabled by default. Hence recovery is failed with below error.

RECOVER DATA finished with error:
recovery could not be completed,
Backup rootkey xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx is required for decryption of backup files, but not found in key store. Please make sure you have provided the necessary keys before recovery., No root key matching hash=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

Resolution :

Please follow below mentioned step by step procedure to complete with recovery with encrypted backup files. We have performed this procedure in terms of system refresh hence the terms source database is Production and Target is Pre-Prod DB.

1. First we need to get the DBID of source and Target Database. Run below sql query in systemdb from hana studio to get the Database ID of Tenant Database.

SELECT DATABASE_NAME,
CASE WHEN (DBID = '' AND
DATABASE_NAME = 'SYSTEMDB')
THEN 1
WHEN (DBID = '' AND
DATABASE_NAME <> 'SYSTEMDB')
THEN 3
ELSE TO_INT(DBID)
END DATABASE_ID
FROM (SELECT DISTINCT DATABASE_NAME, SUBSTR_AFTER (SUBPATH,'.') AS DBID FROM SYS_DATABASES.M_VOLUMES);

Make a note of source and target DBID. e.g DBID of source and Target Tenant is 3.  

2. The keys can be seen at HANA DB OS level using below command with SIDADM user.

hdbnsutil -printHashedRootKeys –dbid=3 --verbose

3. Now we need to take the backup of target (Pre-Prod System) backup keys before changing/replacing it from source system.

  • First we need set the password for keys before taking the backup, this can be set in HANA Cockpit From the Database Overview, with the Security and User Management or All view selected, choose the Data Encryption card. In the Data Encryption app, choose Manage Keys. On the Manage Keys page, click Set Root Key Backup Password and specify the password in tenant database. or can be using below SQL statement - 
    ALTER SYSTEM SET ENCRYPTION ROOT KEYS BACKUP PASSWORD <passphrase>
  • The backup encryption keys backup can be taken from HANA DB OS level using below command with SIDADM user

hdbnsutil -backupRootKeys target_backup.rkb --dbid=3 --type='BACKUP'

The file <target_backup.rkb> will generate at same location with backup of encryption keys 

  • This backup can be validated with previously set password using below command 

hdbnsutil -validateRootKeysBackup target_backup.rkb

This command will ask for password which we had set for keys in HANA cockpit, If it matches it gives successful validation result.

4. Now we have the backup of Target backup encryption keys, The goal of this procedure is to replace the backup encryption keys of target DB with keys from source DB. 

Take the keys screenshot of source DB from HANA DB OS level using below command 

hdbnsutil -printHashedRootKeys --dbid=3 –verbose

5. Set the password for keys in source database. Take key backup and validate as per the steps mentioned in point 3. The keys backup taken in this step has to move to target database server to import 

6. Stop the target DB and import the keys using below OS level command with SIDADM user.

hdbnsutil -recoverRootKeys source_backup.rkb --dbid=3 --type='BACKUP'

This will change only backup keys as per the source database other keys should remain unchanged, this can be validated using the screenshot taken in step 4.

Now source and target is having same backup encryption keys. Start the DB again and resume the recovery in HANA Studio, It should work.

Reference:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003425722

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/7def3297f93842a6b04f4d3f77ae07f6.html

Requesting all readers to drop your feedback or thoughts in comments, also post your questions, if you have any, I will try to answer to the best of my knowledge and research.

Do follow my profile to see more useful contents related to SAP BASIS and HANA topics in near future.

entry.title: Re: SAC login without HANA Cloud Service Account ?
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently, there are two ways to log in:

  1. Your system owner creates a user in SAC. An email will be sent to the entered email address to complete the registration if no account exists in the default SAP IDP.
  2. You log in using SAML SSO, where your account defined in your custom IDP is reused for authentication.

Here you can read more about this process: 

Create Users

Log In for the First Time

I hope that information helps you.

Have a good day!

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports and SQL encrypt connection using OLE...
entry.content:
Hello,

A few things to consider when using CR and TLS 1.2.

First, CR assumes the connection to SQL server is working under TLS 1.2 encryption, it's the Client that is handing the security, not CR, CR uses the Corba layer to communicate to the Client, how the Client talks to the Server is of no concern. The error message returned is from the Client, so clear that first.

Check this KBA for more details on setting up MS SQL Server, note it is old so some or all of the info may be out dated:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2269180

Also note that the reports MUST be updated, you cannot use TLS 1.2 with older MS Clients, Set Location in the Report Designer. MS changed the name of the client dll and the only way for this to work is to first update and then validate connection in CR Designer first. Also I like to verify the Client is configured by testing the connection using an ODBC DSN, if that works then OLD DB connection will work also.

Second, check the PAM for CR for VS and CR Designer, currently OLE DB 19 is not supported:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/f871031e-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

ODBC typically works better but this changes from MS Client version to version, sometimes ODBC works, sometimes OLE DB works....

Your options are to down grade to MS SQL 18 Client first, or test using CR Designer first and save the report if it works.

I'm retired now and I don't recall why R&D has not added MS 19 Client support, likely some bug in the client that R&D is not willing to write code to workaround. History lesson: As of CR 9 R&D rewrote the database Client parts (CRQE.DLL) to remove all of the "bugs" in the various clients, it became to much to maintain so the result is if there's a bug in client it's up to the makers to fix them.

FYI - search for a KBA on how to enable CRLogger to trace CR's database logs

entry.title: SAP BTP trial - how to learn about it and use the trial
entry.content:
Hello - I work for an organization where we were notified about some free BTP trial we could enroll in - to presumably learn about the SAP product.  I enrolled and can "access" the trial system - but no idea what to do there.  Is there any sort of guide or documentation on what to do?  Or are you expected to just figure it out on your own.  Please advise.  (I already went thru the people in my organization that offered this trial up - and first they had me log a ticket to their team - and then they just referred me to this site and told me to ask here)

==

Hands on Trial Versions:-

... has partnered with SAP to offer trial versions of SAP BTP and SAP Analytics Cloud. To access these environments, please complete your registration. Make sure you have a valid S-User ID/Universal ID to benefit from these free trials.

entry.title: Re: Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud as a part of S4/HANA system
entry.content:
Hello!

Related to your question:

Q: Does the “embedded scenario” require additional licenses?
A: No, the embedded SAP Analytics Cloud features are covered by the existing SAP S/4HANA Cloud license.

This information was previously allocated in the official documentation on "Integrated Analytics: SAP Analytics Cloud now embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud", where you can read more about this topic.

You can also read more about this topic in the documentation below:

  • Integrated Analytics FAQ
  • Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Get Empowered!

I hope this helps you.

Have a nice day

entry.title: Integration of S/4HANA Public Cloud with the Online Validation Service: List of VAT Payers
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to provide a quick guide on how to setup the system integration required for the “Online Validation – List of VAT Payers” scenario for country-specific requirements in Poland. The Online Validation service is used to check the business partner tax registration number and bank account number against the List of VAT payers published by the Polish tax authorities. An overview of the scenario can be found in the official product documentation here.

Once the prerequisites mentioned in the documentation are fulfilled, here is an overview of the steps required to setup and implement the Online Validation Service:

BTP Steps

  1. Open the integration package guide for the technical setup of the Online Validation service.
  2. The first step is to download and deploy a Java Application on the BTP subaccount as described in this GitHub repository. Note that you will need the Cloud IDE Business Application Studio (BAS) to complete this step.
  3. Follow the steps explained in the GitHub documentation: the only additional step to be aware of is to change one line of code before executing the installation script. In line 4 of the install.sh file, append ‘—sso’ to the cf login command. Furthermore, in line 3, make sure to replace the example endpoint provided from the script with the real api endpoint which can be found in your BTP subaccount’s Cloud Foundry Environment overview section. In the demo subaccount used for this blog, the example endpoint from the script https://api.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com will be replaced as shown below:
 

CPI Steps

Download the integration packages from the Integration Suite as shown here. Next, configure and deploy the necessary iFlows as described here.

Apart from the steps explained in the documentation, there are two key points to make a note of. In the iFlow “Polish List of VAT Payers Validation Service”, there is no authentication needed for the HTTP call made to the BTP Java application:

 

Secondly, in the “Route Validation Request to Online Validation Service” iFlow, it is important to change the User Role to ‘ESBMessaging.send’ in the “Configure” section.

S/4HANA Cloud Steps

At this point, the last step is the communication management setup on S/4HC side. For this, follow the steps as described here. For outbound authentication, you will need to create a service key in the VAT whitelist app binding instance on the BTP subaccount. The service key Client ID/Client secret will then be used as the outbound user credentials.

Now, the setup and configuration steps on all systems has been covered. The BTP Java application will have been deployed in a Cloud Foundry space and the CPI iFlows will have been configured and deployed as described in the documentation. Now, the final step is to execute the online validation from the S/4HC system and see the end result. For this, use the app “Online Validation for Multiple Partners”, with the company code for Poland and the Check Identification ‘PLWL’ as selection criteria:

 

Upon executing the run, the Online Validation Service will be triggered in the background and you should see the following result:

Result Code ‘1’ represent a successful validation, ‘3’ represents an invalid check result and ‘4’ signifies that the bank account details have not been maintained correctly in the business partner master data. With this, the integration between S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and the Online Validation service for the List of VAT Payers in Poland has been successfully setup and can be used to validate the compliance of business partners with the local country-specific regulations.

entry.title: Re: Turn OFF Email Notifications from SAP Business Networ...
entry.content:
Hello Velu,

From a supplier's perspective, and when transactions are taking place through SAP Business Network, the suppliers themselves manage the notifications they wish to receive or not. 

Moving your suppliers to the network is a great way to transact with them with renewed efficiency and transparency, allowing them to not only secure smooth business with you, but also grow their portfolio by transacting with other customers, all while using the same supplier account - entirely for free in the case of a standard account.

Notifications can be toggled on and off based on document type, but once again I believe this can only be done from the supplier account.

I hope this is helpful, and wish you successful transacting with your suppliers.

Benjamin

entry.title: Re: Categories can contain either other categories or pro...
entry.content:
No, this is not correct. In SAP Hybris, Categories can contain both products and subcategories simultaneously.

  • Categories in Hybris are hierarchical structures that allow you to organize products and subcategories.
  • A single category can:
    • Contain products directly assigned to it.
    • Have subcategories, which can themselves contain other subcategories or products

entry.title: Add SAP GUI Transaction in Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
You can expose SAP GUI transactions in the Fiori Launchpad to provide users with a unified and modern interface for accessing both new Fiori apps and traditional transactions. Here's a breakdown of the process:

1. Verify Transaction Type:

Ensure the transaction is compatible with SAP GUI for HTML. You can check this using transaction SE93.

2. Create a Semantic Object:

Use transaction `/n/UI2/SEMOBJ` to create a semantic object for the transaction. This object acts as a link between the Fiori Launchpad and the transaction.
A semantic object is a technical name that represents a business entity (e.g., Sales Order, Purchase Order).

3. Use the Fiori Launchpad Designer:

Access the Fiori Launchpad Designer (FLP Designer).

4. Create a Catalog (if needed):

In the "Catalog" tab, create a new custom catalog or use an existing one to organize your transactions.

5. Create a Tile:

In the catalog, create a new tile of type "App Launcher - Static".
Link the tile to the semantic object you created earlier.
Provide a title, subtitle, and icon for the tile.

6. Create a Target Mapping:

Create a target mapping to define how the tile will launch the transaction.
Specify the transaction code and any necessary parameters.

7. Create a Group:

Create a group to which the tile will be assigned. Groups are used to organize tiles in the Launchpad.

8. Assign the Group to a Role:

Work with your security team to assign the group to a role. This controls which users can see and access the tile.

9. Test in the Fiori Launchpad:

Open the Fiori Launchpad and verify that the tile appears and launches the transaction correctly.

Important Considerations:

Security: Ensure proper authorization checks are in place to restrict access to sensitive transactions.
User Experience: While this method allows you to expose transactions in Fiori, consider whether a Fiori app is available or if developing one would provide a better user experience.
Parameters: You can pass parameters from the Fiori Launchpad to the transaction to pre-populate fields or control its behavior.

By following these steps, you can effectively integrate SAP GUI transactions into the Fiori Launchpad, providing users with a more streamlined and modern way to access essential business functions.

https://youtu.be/VMf-NtbeLBI

entry.title: Re: how to consume ODATA while performing CRUD operation...
entry.content:
Hi. @shrutikah

"Employees" entity set is read-only.
so Create, Update, and Delete operation are not possible,
only Read operation is possible.

For more information, please refer to the link below.
https://services.odata.org/v2/

Additionally,
OData Protocol exists up to V2, V3, and V4.
but SAPUI5 Framework only supports V2 and V4.

I hope for good results!

entry.title: SAP CAP CDS simple question
entry.content:
I have a question about the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) framework. I'm curious about the reasoning behind its folder structure and why it seems challenging to personalize the routes. As a newcomer to this framework, I wonder why the CAP team designed it this way.

For example, with Mongoose, you only need to create the model (schema) and the connection with MongoDB. Everything else, like routes, can be created using Express, which seems to offer more flexibility for creating a REST API compared to CAP. While CAP requires less code and allows for data validation in the body for POST requests, it also seems more rigid. For instance, in CAP, I can make a request to the same route using GET or POST and get the same result. Why is that? In Express, if I make a GET request to a route that only accepts POST, it won't enter the route because the method doesn't match. How would I achieve that in CAP?

Again, I am very new to this, so my question might not be entirely relevant. Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add custom field in CVD1 Tcode to send email for selected rows

Hello

regarding:

"Hello Christopher, thanks for your response, My requirement is to resend SDS document over the mail for selected row in CVD1. Thanks."

As mentioned: may be you can use "Enhancement Point" to achieve this.. but a " customer" approach is better

By using ABAP: You need to select the report shipment order.. and then you can "resent" (under some conditions) the SDS (either per eMail or Letter or)

But in most cases: it is "preferred" to use the "MAN_CALL" based on input via CVD1 to do the job as here you need "zero programming" and you need "only" to understand CVD1 and CG54 options to get a "MAN_CALL" solution

I would go for this solution (as simple, no develoment needed (but may be configuration), easy to use etc.)

Wish you the best for 2025

C.B.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP trial - how to learn about it and use the trial
entry.content:
BTP is a whole new world! Everything you know about SAP from the GUI is now transported to a cloud-based environment. Reading about it on the SAP website can be overwhelming, so I recommend taking an online course from a third-party provider in India. They offer demos that give you a comprehensive view of the entire landscape.

I’m currently taking one of these courses, and you can create a BTP trial account without linking it to your corporate account. If you remain active, the account will extend beyond the initial 30 days. Once you’re in your Global Account, you can explore various features like subscribing to services, configuring the ALM and Event Mesh, Workzone Builder,  setting up API integrations, and managing identity providers, including integration with your corporate identity provider.  You can access SAP Learning Hub to take on the free courses  - https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/develop-advanced-extensions-with-sap-cloud-sdk/exercise-creating-your-free-trial-account-in-sap-btp_b8fa6059-4171-4025-b9c6-1f7ca747ab83

There’s so much to discover! #BTP #BTPSecurity, #BTPEA, #BTPTrial
